answer:
<think> The Northern Crusades were religious wars undertaken by Catholic Christian military orders and kingdoms. A sacred grove or sacred woods are any grove of trees that are of special religious importance to a particular culture. It's reasonable to assume that during the Northern Crusades, if the invading forces came across a sacred grove or woods that were important to the local culture, they may have built churches on those sites as a way to assert their religious dominance or to convert the local population. </think>

<answer> sacred groves or woods </answer>

<answer> sites of sacred groves or woods </answer>

<answer> places where churches were built during the Northern Crusades </answer>

<answer> churches </answer>

<answer> \boxed{churches} </answer>
